text.title: Pair Programming Anti-patterns
text.summary: Human-human and AI-specific pairing failures: keyboard hog, backseat driver, disengaged observer, remote fatigue, agent instruction creep.

antipattern.description: Keyboard hog — one person always drives, never switches
antipattern.reason: The non-driver disengages and loses context. Use timer-based switching (25 min).

antipattern.description: Backseat driver — navigator dictates every keystroke instead of guiding strategy
antipattern.reason: Micromanagement frustrates the driver and defeats the purpose of role separation. Trust the driver at the tactical level.

antipattern.description: Disengaged observer — navigator zones out, checks phone, multitasks
antipattern.reason: Disengaged navigator provides no value. Use active techniques (Ping-Pong) to keep both participants engaged.

antipattern.description: Forced pairing on everything including trivial tasks
antipattern.reason: Pairing overhead exceeds benefit for boilerplate. Choose tasks wisely — pair on complex or knowledge-transfer situations.

antipattern.description: Silent pairing — no communication between driver and navigator
antipattern.reason: Silent pairing is just two people sitting together, not pairing. Use the think-aloud protocol.

antipattern.description: Agent emitting full files instead of bounded instructions under length pressure
antipattern.reason: Full-file output removes the human from the loop and turns the session into a review exercise. Restate Strong-Style rules every ~10 turns.

antipattern.description: Navigator pollution — agent fills silence with unsolicited rewrites during exploration
antipattern.reason: Unsolicited suggestions interrupt the human's thinking. Explicitly mute the agent: "only answer when I ask".

antipattern.description: Agent auto-committing work during a pair session
antipattern.reason: Every commit during pairing must be reviewed by the human. Agents should propose; humans decide and commit.

antipattern.description: Using an IDE tab-completer (Copilot, Cursor) as a substitute for strategic pairing with a reasoning model
antipattern.reason: Tab completers operate at line level. A reasoning model (Claude) is better as a strategic navigator on architecture. Use the right tool for each layer.
